description: Curated Gene Sleep SNP panel for Yeliztli (P3-48/P3-49). Covers 4 sleep pathways (Caffeine & Sleep, Chronotype & Circadian Rhythm, Sleep Quality, Sleep Disorders) with 6 curated SNPs plus GWAS Catalog sleep/circadian EFO term associations. CYP1A2 rs762551 has cross-module reference to Pharmacogenomics. PER3 rs57875989 is a VNTR proxy with coverage caveat. rs2858884 is retained as a non-diagnostic HLA-DQ region marker only — it is NOT a validated HLA-DQB1*06:02 / narcolepsy-risk proxy and no narcolepsy risk is inferred from it.

pathways.name: Caffeine & Sleep
pathways.description: Variants influencing caffeine metabolism rate and adenosine receptor sensitivity, both of which affect caffeine-induced sleep disruption.

AA.effect_summary: rs762551 AA (A-allele homozygote) - rapid/high-inducibility caffeine-metabolism context. Caffeine is cleared quickly, reducing the likelihood of caffeine-related sleep disruption.

AC.effect_summary: rs762551 AC (A/C heterozygote) - intermediate caffeine-metabolism context. Moderate caffeine clearance rate. Evening caffeine intake may mildly affect sleep onset.

CA.effect_summary: rs762551 CA (A/C heterozygote) - intermediate caffeine-metabolism context. Moderate caffeine clearance rate. Evening caffeine intake may mildly affect sleep onset.

CC.effect_summary: rs762551 CC (C-allele homozygote) - slower caffeine-metabolism context. Caffeine persists longer in the body, increasing the risk of caffeine-induced insomnia and delayed sleep onset.

snps.coverage_note: The Sleep panel uses rs762551 only as a caffeine-metabolism marker; it is not a full CYP1A2 star-allele caller and does not report CYP1A2 diplotypes. PharmVar now assigns the former CYP1A2*1F-defining -163C>A core variant to the CYP1A2*30 allele group, and rs762551 can occur on multiple CYP1A2 haplotypes.

cross_module.note: CYP1A2 is also a pharmacogene relevant to drug metabolism (e.g., clozapine, theophylline). See Pharmacogenomics module for full drug interaction profile and star-allele interpretation.

snps.recommendation_text: CYP1A2 determines caffeine half-life. Slow metabolizers may benefit from limiting caffeine intake to morning hours (before noon) to minimize sleep disruption. This variant also has pharmacogenomic relevance — see the Pharmacogenomics module.

CC.effect_summary: Standard adenosine A2A receptor sensitivity. Typical caffeine-related alertness and sleep effects.

CT.effect_summary: One copy of the T allele. Modestly increased sensitivity to caffeine's effects on sleep architecture and anxiety.

TC.effect_summary: One copy of the T allele. Modestly increased sensitivity to caffeine's effects on sleep architecture and anxiety.

TT.effect_summary: Homozygous T allele. Increased adenosine A2A receptor sensitivity. Associated with greater caffeine-induced anxiety and sleep disruption, even at moderate doses.

snps.recommendation_text: ADORA2A encodes the adenosine A2A receptor — the primary target of caffeine. TT carriers may be more sensitive to caffeine's anxiogenic and sleep-disrupting effects. Consider reducing total caffeine intake, not just timing.

pathways.name: Chronotype & Circadian Rhythm
pathways.description: Variants influencing intrinsic circadian clock period length and chronotype preference (morningness vs. eveningness).

GG.effect_summary: Proxy for PER3 5-repeat VNTR allele. Associated with morningness — earlier sleep onset and wake times. Standard circadian alignment.

GA.effect_summary: Heterozygous PER3 VNTR proxy. Intermediate chronotype. No strong bias toward morningness or eveningness.

AG.effect_summary: Heterozygous PER3 VNTR proxy. Intermediate chronotype. No strong bias toward morningness or eveningness.

AA.effect_summary: Proxy for PER3 4-repeat VNTR allele. Associated with eveningness — later preferred sleep and wake times. May experience delayed sleep phase tendency.

snps.coverage_note: rs57875989 is a proxy SNP for the PER3 54-bp variable number tandem repeat (VNTR) polymorphism (4-repeat vs. 5-repeat), which is not directly genotyped on SNP arrays. Proxy accuracy depends on linkage disequilibrium in the individual's ancestry population.
snps.recommendation_text: PER3 VNTR influences circadian period length. Individuals with the 4-repeat allele (eveningness) may benefit from consistent sleep schedules and morning light exposure to maintain circadian alignment.

pathways.name: Sleep Quality
pathways.description: Variants influencing restless legs syndrome (RLS) susceptibility and periodic limb movements during sleep, which can significantly disrupt sleep quality.

TT.effect_summary: Standard MEIS1 expression. Lower baseline risk for restless legs syndrome.

TG.effect_summary: One copy of the RLS-associated G allele. Mildly increased susceptibility to restless legs syndrome and periodic limb movements during sleep.

GT.effect_summary: One copy of the RLS-associated G allele. Mildly increased susceptibility to restless legs syndrome and periodic limb movements during sleep.

GG.effect_summary: Homozygous RLS risk allele. Significantly increased susceptibility to restless legs syndrome. MEIS1 is the strongest genetic risk factor for RLS identified to date.

snps.recommendation_text: MEIS1 is the strongest known genetic risk factor for restless legs syndrome (RLS). GG carriers with RLS symptoms should consult a sleep specialist. Iron status evaluation may be warranted, as iron deficiency can exacerbate RLS.

CC.effect_summary: Standard BTBD9 expression. Lower baseline risk for periodic limb movements during sleep.

CT.effect_summary: One copy of the PLMS-associated T allele. Mildly increased susceptibility to periodic limb movements during sleep and restless legs syndrome.

TC.effect_summary: One copy of the PLMS-associated T allele. Mildly increased susceptibility to periodic limb movements during sleep and restless legs syndrome.

TT.effect_summary: Homozygous PLMS risk allele. Increased susceptibility to periodic limb movements during sleep. BTBD9 variants also associated with altered iron metabolism in the brain.

snps.recommendation_text: BTBD9 is associated with periodic limb movements during sleep (PLMS) and RLS. BTBD9 may influence brain iron metabolism. If sleep is frequently disrupted, a sleep study can evaluate for PLMS.

pathways.name: Sleep Disorders
pathways.description: HLA class II region marker for narcolepsy context. Narcolepsy type 1 genetic risk is driven by HLA-DQB1*06:02, which is not directly typed by SNP-array panels; no validated SNP-array proxy is currently included.

snps.variant_name: HLA-DQ region marker (rs2858884) — not a DQB1*06:02 proxy

CC.effect_summary: rs2858884 is an HLA class II region marker, not a validated proxy for HLA-DQB1*06:02 carriage. No narcolepsy genotype call is made. Narcolepsy type 1 risk is driven by HLA-DQB1*06:02, which this SNP does not tag and which requires direct HLA typing.

CT.effect_summary: rs2858884 is an HLA class II region marker, not a validated proxy for HLA-DQB1*06:02 carriage. No narcolepsy genotype call is made. Narcolepsy type 1 risk is driven by HLA-DQB1*06:02, which this SNP does not tag and which requires direct HLA typing.

TC.effect_summary: rs2858884 is an HLA class II region marker, not a validated proxy for HLA-DQB1*06:02 carriage. No narcolepsy genotype call is made. Narcolepsy type 1 risk is driven by HLA-DQB1*06:02, which this SNP does not tag and which requires direct HLA typing.

TT.effect_summary: rs2858884 is an HLA class II region marker, not a validated proxy for HLA-DQB1*06:02 carriage. No narcolepsy genotype call is made. Narcolepsy type 1 risk is driven by HLA-DQB1*06:02, which this SNP does not tag and which requires direct HLA typing.

snps.coverage_note: rs2858884 was previously curated as a tag-SNP proxy for HLA-DQB1*06:02 and narcolepsy type 1 risk. That is not scientifically supported. The GWAS that identified rs2858884 in narcolepsy (Hor et al. 2010, Nat Genet, PMID 20711174) matched cases and controls on HLA-DRB1*15:01-DQB1*06:02, so rs2858884 cannot tag DQB1*06:02 carriage; its associated HLA-region signal is protective and linked to other haplotypes (DRB1*03-DQB1*02 and DRB1*13:01-DQB1*06:03), and is interpretable only conditional on DQB1*06:02 status. Direct HLA-DQB1*06:02 typing (Tafti et al. 2014, Sleep, PMID 24381371) — not an array tag SNP — is required for narcolepsy genetic risk. This SNP is retained as a non-diagnostic HLA-region marker only; no narcolepsy risk is inferred from any genotype.
snps.recommendation_text: Narcolepsy type 1 is strongly associated with HLA-DQB1*06:02, but that allele is not directly typed by SNP-array panels and rs2858884 is not a valid substitute for it. No narcolepsy risk is inferred from this marker. If excessive daytime sleepiness, cataplexy, or other narcolepsy symptoms are present, consult a sleep specialist for clinical evaluation, including direct HLA-DQB1*06:02 typing, polysomnography, and MSLT.

CYP1A2_pgx_context.note: CYP1A2 rs762551 appears under Caffeine & Sleep (slow metabolizer → sleep disruption), but is also a pharmacogene affecting metabolism of clozapine, theophylline, and other drugs. The Pharmacogenomics module provides the full drug interaction profile — Sleep module references but does not re-compute PGx findings.

CYP1A2_metabolizer.description: Caffeine metabolizer status based on CYP1A2 rs762551 (-163C>A) genotype. This is not a full CYP1A2 star-allele call and does not emit CYP1A2 diplotypes.

rapid.label: Rapid metabolizer
rapid.description: rs762551 AA (A-allele homozygote) - fast caffeine clearance. Low risk of caffeine-induced sleep disruption.

intermediate.label: Intermediate metabolizer
intermediate.description: rs762551 AC/CA (A/C heterozygote) - moderate caffeine clearance. Some sensitivity to evening caffeine.

slow.label: Slow metabolizer
slow.description: rs762551 CC (C-allele homozygote) - slower caffeine clearance. Higher risk of caffeine-induced insomnia.

HLA_DQ_region_marker.description: HLA-DQB1*06:02 is the primary HLA allele associated with narcolepsy type 1, but it is not directly typed by SNP-array panels. rs2858884 is an HLA-DQ region marker, NOT a validated proxy for HLA-DQB1*06:02 carriage.
HLA_DQ_region_marker.proxy_accuracy_note: rs2858884 is not a valid proxy for HLA-DQB1*06:02. The defining narcolepsy GWAS (Hor et al. 2010, PMID 20711174) matched cases and controls on HLA-DRB1*15:01-DQB1*06:02, so this SNP cannot tag DQB1*06:02 carriage; its associated HLA-region signal is protective and conditional on DQB1*06:02 status. No narcolepsy risk is inferred from this marker; direct HLA-DQB1*06:02 typing (PMID 24381371) is required for narcolepsy genetic risk assessment.

PER3_VNTR_proxy.description: PER3 variable number tandem repeat (54-bp VNTR, 4-repeat vs. 5-repeat) is not directly genotyped on SNP arrays. rs57875989 is used as a proxy SNP in linkage disequilibrium with the VNTR.
PER3_VNTR_proxy.proxy_accuracy_note: Linkage disequilibrium between rs57875989 and PER3 VNTR varies by ancestry. Proxy accuracy is highest in European populations and may be lower in other populations. Results should be interpreted with this caveat.
